title: Lepidanthus suaveolens
intro: Lepidanthus suaveolens, also known as Lavender Sage, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. The bioactive constituents of Lepidanthus suaveolens include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to calm the nervous system and promote a restful night's sleep.
taxonomy: Lepidanthus suaveolens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Lepidanthus, which is a small group of plants native to South America. Lepidanthus suaveolens is further categorized as a member of the tribe Mutisieae, within the Asteraceae family. This classification helps identify the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics. The specific classification of Lepidanthus suaveolens is essential for understanding its unique features and behaviors.
